nodes.label: preCICE Knowledge Base Query Policy (root CLAUDE.md)
nodes.rationale: Mandates calling kb_query_precice_live before answering any preCICE question instead of relying on training data, because the tool auto-ingests and caches for 1 hour, keeping answers grounded and fast on repeat queries.
nodes.norm_label: precice knowledge base query policy (root claude.md)

nodes.label: graphify Optional Codebase Graph Rule (root CLAUDE.md)
nodes.rationale: Instructs querying graphify-out/graph.json for codebase questions when present, and updating it after code changes, to avoid stale or redundant full-file exploration.
nodes.norm_label: graphify optional codebase graph rule (root claude.md)

nodes.label: AGENTS.md Safety Rules
nodes.rationale: Restricts destructive shell commands and file deletion, requires inspecting and backing up precice-config.xml before modification, and prefers MCP tools over raw shell access to keep agent actions safe and reversible.

nodes.label: README Safety Notes
nodes.rationale: Documents that run_command_in_project is restricted to a prefix allowlist and blocks destructive patterns (rm, sudo, curl, wget, fork bombs) so agent-driven shell execution stays contained to test-projects.

nodes.label: Approach 2: LangChain/LangGraph Agent
nodes.author: Vaibhav Dange
nodes.rationale: Considered for model-agnostic, auditable multi-step reasoning with built-in memory, but rejected as primary layer due to framework overhead and abstraction churn.

nodes.label: Approach 3: Raw Function Calling (Anthropic/OpenAI API)
nodes.author: Vaibhav Dange
nodes.rationale: Valued as a transparent thesis baseline demonstrating tool-calling fundamentals without framework magic, but scales poorly as tool count grows.

nodes.label: Approach 4: Semantic Kernel (Microsoft)
nodes.author: Vaibhav Dange
nodes.rationale: Rejected for this project because it is Azure-centric and less suited to academic Linux/HPC environments where preCICE typically runs.

nodes.label: Approach 5: Fine-Tuned Domain LLM
nodes.author: Vaibhav Dange
nodes.rationale: Offers highest thesis novelty and offline HPC deployment, but rejected as the primary approach due to data-collection burden, GPU cost, and inability to execute simulations on its own.

nodes.label: Approach A: Obsidian Vault + MCP Write-back
nodes.author: Vaibhav Dange
nodes.rationale: Chosen for human-AI collaborative knowledge management because it is Git-trackable, human-readable, and doubles as living thesis documentation.
nodes.norm_label: approach a: obsidian vault + mcp write-back

nodes.label: Approach B: Vector Database RAG Pipeline
nodes.author: Vaibhav Dange
nodes.rationale: Chosen for document knowledge because preCICE's documentation corpus benefits from semantic retrieval and ChromaDB is lightweight, local, and requires no cloud infrastructure.
nodes.norm_label: approach b: vector database rag pipeline

nodes.label: Approach C: mem0 Agent Memory Layer
nodes.author: Vaibhav Dange
nodes.rationale: Chosen for experiential session memory because it is simpler to integrate than a custom memory system and complements RAG's static document knowledge.
nodes.norm_label: approach c: mem0 agent memory layer

nodes.label: Approach D: GraphRAG / Knowledge Graph
nodes.author: Vaibhav Dange
nodes.rationale: Recognized as a natural fit for preCICE's relational participant-mesh-data-mapping structure, but deferred due to high construction and maintenance cost versus flat RAG for most queries.

nodes.label: Approach E: Fine-Tuned Embeddings
nodes.author: Vaibhav Dange
nodes.rationale: Would improve retrieval precision on preCICE jargon, but engineering overhead and training-data requirements were judged not to justify the marginal gain for a thesis.

nodes.label: Recommended Stack: MCP + ChromaDB RAG + Obsidian + mem0
nodes.author: Vaibhav Dange
nodes.rationale: A layered hybrid was chosen over any single approach because execution (MCP), document knowledge (RAG), experiential memory (mem0), and human documentation (Obsidian) are each best solved by a different tool, and the layering enables incremental, evaluable ablation studies for the thesis.
nodes.norm_label: recommended stack: mcp + chromadb rag + obsidian + mem0

nodes.label: PreToolUse Grep-to-Graphify Hook
nodes.rationale: Intercepts Bash calls to grep/rg/find/fd/ack/ag and injects a reminder to use graphify query instead when graphify-out/graph.json exists, steering the agent toward the cheaper scoped-subgraph lookup over raw-file search.
